prompt: Translate the following English sentence into French, Japanese, and Arabic, and add a brief note on any phrase that was tricky to render faithfully: "The silence between us said more than any argument could have."
response:
French: "Le silence entre nous a dit plus que tout argument ne pourrait le faire."

Japanese: "私たちの中の沈黙は、どの論争よりも多を語っている。"

Arabic: "الصمت بيننا قال أكثر مما يمكن للجدال أن يقول."

Note: The phrase "said more than" could potentially be tricky when translating into Japanese and Arabic. In English, it means that the silence between us conveyed more meaning or had a greater impact than any argument could have. In Japanese, I used "を語っている" to indicate that the silence was speaking more than any argument could. In Arabic, I used "قال أكثر مما" to convey the same idea. The rest of the sentence is straightforward in terms of translation.
